mappings.rationaleAI: A core text on all types of taxes (income, sales, capital gains, corporate, excise), tax burdens, progressive vs. regressive taxes, and tax systems (B.2, B.3).
Explores the measurement and distribution of income and wealth including GDP and factor shares (A.1, A.2).
Directly addresses income distribution, inequality, housing, and the conditions at the lower end of the wealth spectrum (A.3).
Government-administered welfare programs, social services, unemployment insurance, and transfers are central themes (B.4.c).
Examines how wages, profits, interest, and rent generate income distribution patterns under capitalism (A.2.a).
Traces historical patterns of wealth distribution among countries and the causes of inequality (A.3).
Antitrust legislation and regulation of monopolistic practices function as direct controls over the private sector (B.6.b).
Wage controls, equal pay, and labour legislation directly affect income distribution (B.6.a, B.6.b).
Keynesian fiscal policy -- budgetary deficits and surpluses, public debt, and government spending -- is essential for understanding state intervention in income distribution (B.1, B.5).
Debates about taxation, deregulation, and privatization as government approaches to income distribution are examined.
Income inequality between nations and purchasing power parity comparisons across countries are key themes (A.3).
Marx's critique of wealth distribution under capitalism and the relationship between capital and labour income remains foundational (A.2.a).
Socialist approaches to redistribution through public ownership, social democracy, and progressive taxation are central themes (B).
Smith's classical theories of wealth distribution through factor shares -- wages, rent, and profits -- laid the groundwork for modern economics (A.2.a).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationaleAI: Interest payments, public debt, and financial mechanisms through which government affects income are key topics (B.4.a, B.5).

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationaleAI: The New Deal serves as a landmark case study of government transfers, subsidies, and fiscal policy affecting income distribution (B.4, B.5).
Explores how government economic policy and taxation interact with actual economic behaviour (B.2, B.4.b).
